name: Czechia
topIncomeNote: Top rate of 23% with no sub-central income tax (Tax Foundation combined figure for 2026 is also 23.0%).
corporateNote: 21% applies to tax periods starting in 2024 (was 19% before then), per PwC.
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 33.2%).

name: Estonia
topIncomeNote: Flat 22% on all resident income (PwC, reviewed May 2026); Tax Foundation's 2026 combined figure is also 22.0%.
corporateNote: Distribution-based system: 22% applies only when profits are distributed; retained earnings are untaxed (PwC).
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 33.6%).

name: Greece
topIncomeNote: Top marginal rate of 44%; no sub-central income tax (Tax Foundation combined figure for 2026 is also 44.0%).
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 38.9%).

name: Hungary
topIncomeNote: Flat 15% personal income tax; no sub-central income tax (Tax Foundation combined figure for 2026 is also 15.0%).
corporateNote: Lowest headline corporate rate in the OECD (Tax Foundation 2025 combined figure: 9.0%).
vatNote: Highest standard VAT rate in the OECD; per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 35.0%).

OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 - Tax revenue trends 1965–2024

name: Italy
topIncomeNote: 43% national IRPEF top rate plus regional surcharge (1.23%–3.33%) and municipal surcharge (0%–0.9%); 47.2% is the Tax Foundation 2026 combined central + sub-central figure.
corporateNote: 24% IRES (PwC) plus the standard 3.9% regional production tax (IRAP); 27.8% is the Tax Foundation 2025 combined figure (27.81%). IRAP varies by region and industry.
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 41.5%).

name: Latvia
topIncomeNote: Brackets of 25.5% (up to EUR 105,300) and 33%, plus an additional 3% on total annual income above EUR 200,000, giving a 36% top marginal rate (PwC; Tax Foundation 2026 figure also 36.0%).
corporateNote: Distribution-based system: 20% payable only when profits are distributed (PwC).
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 32.5%); largest 2024 increase in the OECD (+2.4 p.p.).

name: Lithuania
topIncomeNote: 32% top rate on employment income; different rates apply to individual-activity and other non-employment income (PwC). Tax Foundation 2026 combined figure also 32.0%.
corporateNote: Standard rate 17% from 1 January 2026 (PwC); Tax Foundation lists 16% for 2025. Credit institutions pay an additional 5%.
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 32.1%).

name: Poland
topIncomeNote: 32% top bracket plus a 4% solidarity levy on income above PLN 1 million, giving a 36% combined top rate (PwC; Tax Foundation 2026 figure also 36.0%).
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 34.9%).

name: Portugal
topIncomeNote: 48% top bracket plus solidarity surtax of 2.5% on taxable income above EUR 80,000 and 5% above EUR 250,000, giving a 53% combined top rate (PwC; Tax Foundation 2026 figure also 53.0%).
corporateNote: Maximum combined rate for 2026: 19% headline IRC (mainland) plus municipal surtax (derrama municipal, up to 1.5%) and state surtax (derrama estadual, up to 9% on profits above EUR 35 million), per PwC. Tax Foundation's 2025 combined figure was 30.5% (20% headline that year).
vatNote: Mainland standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 35.3%).

name: Slovakia
topIncomeNote: From 1 January 2026 new 30% and 35% top brackets apply above multiples of the subsistence minimum (top rate was 25% through 2025), per PwC; Tax Foundation 2026 figure also 35.0%.
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 35.1%). OECD reports Slovak data on a gross basis.

vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 36.4%); second-largest 2024 increase in the OECD (+1.9 p.p.).

name: Spain
topIncomeNote: Top rates vary by autonomous community: the combined rate is the sum of the state scale and each region's own scale. 47% is PwC's headline combined top rate (state withholding scale, income above EUR 300,000). Tax Foundation's 2026 figure is 54.0%, reflecting the highest regional combination.
corporateNote: 25% standard rate (PwC CIT quick chart; Tax Foundation 2025 combined figure also 25.0%).
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 36.4%).

name: Turkey
topIncomeNote: 40% top income tax bracket (above TRY 5,300,000 in 2026) plus stamp duty of 0.759% on gross salaries (PwC); 40.8% is the Tax Foundation 2026 combined-with-surtaxes figure.
corporateNote: 25% standard rate; 30% for financial sector companies (PwC CIT quick chart).
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 23.2%). OECD notes 2024 nominal revenues rose 69% amid very high inflation.

name: Israel
topIncomeNote: 47% top bracket (above ILS 560,280 in 2026) plus 3% surtax on annual taxable income above ILS 721,560, giving a 50% combined top rate (PwC). An additional 2% surtax applies to high capital income from 2025.
vatNote: Standard rate per PwC WWTS VAT rates quick chart (taxsummaries.pwc.com/quick-charts/value-added-tax-vat-rates).
taxToGdpNote: Provisional 2024 figure from OECD Revenue Statistics 2025 (2023 final: 29.8%).
